cta_copy.rule: Button copy = verb + value. Never just a verb. Never just a noun.

patterns.why: Submit is mechanical. The action has a human purpose.
OK is ambiguous and lazy. Be specific about what the user is confirming.
Generic. Name the concrete action and remove friction with 'free'.
Screen readers list links by text — 'click here' means nothing out of context.
Sign up is old-fashioned. 'Create account' sets expectations about what happens next.
Learn more is overused and vague. Specific framing reduces hesitation.
Tell the user what happens when they click. Removes anxiety about data loss.
Upgrade feels like a tax. Unlocking a benefit is motivating.
Scoped destructive copy makes it clear exactly what will be destroyed.
Never confirm destructive actions with ambiguous Yes/No. Name the action.

error_messages.anatomy: [What went wrong] + [Why it went wrong (if helpful)] + [How to fix it]
rules: Never blame the user — 'Invalid email' not 'You entered an invalid email'
Never show technical error codes to users without a human translation
Be specific about the field and the fix — 'Password must be at least 8 characters' not 'Invalid password'
In form validation: present tense, plain language, actionable fix
In empty states / fetch errors: what went wrong + 1-click recovery path

patterns.good: Couldn't save your changes. Check your connection and try again.
Email address is required. Enter a valid email like name@example.com.
You don't have permission to view this. Contact your admin to request access.
Password must be at least 8 characters and include a number.
Couldn't connect. Check your internet connection, then try again.

patterns.bad: Something went wrong. Please try again.
patterns.good: Couldn't load your dashboard. Try refreshing the page.

empty_state_copy.rule: Empty states are a conversation with the user — name what's missing, why, and what to do.

patterns.headline: No [items] yet
patterns.sub: Create your first [item] to get started.

patterns.headline: No results for '[query]'
patterns.sub: Try different keywords or clear the filters.

patterns.headline: Couldn't load [content]
patterns.sub: Check your connection or try again.

patterns.headline: You need access to view this
patterns.sub: Ask your admin to invite you.

toast_copy.rule: Toast messages are 4–8 words. Present tense. Specific. No exclamation marks on errors.

examples: Upload failed. Check your file size.

rules: Placeholder text is supplementary — not a replacement for a visible label
Input placeholder should show format (e.g., 'name@example.com') or a useful example (e.g., 'Search by name or email')
NEVER use placeholder for instructions — they disappear on focus and fail accessibility
Helper text below the label: for persistent format hints (e.g., 'Must be at least 8 characters')
Character count: show below the input, especially when limit is < 280 characters

anatomy.title: Short, specific action: 'Delete project?' or 'Remove team member?'
anatomy.body: Exactly what will happen. What data is lost. Whether it can be undone.
anatomy.cancel_button: Always present: 'Cancel' (or 'Keep project' for extra reassurance)

rules: Destructive confirmation button is always in danger color (bg-danger text-white)
Destructive button is on the RIGHT, cancel on LEFT
For high-stakes actions (delete account, bulk delete), require typing confirmation: 'Type [item name] to confirm'
Never auto-confirm after timeout — always require explicit user action

welcome_screen.headline: One sentence: what the product does for the user
welcome_screen.sub: One sentence: the first action they should take or the key benefit
welcome_screen.cta: Verb + value: 'Set up your workspace' not 'Continue'
welcome_screen.rule: The welcome screen is not for explaining features — it's for setting a direction. Cut everything else.

step_labels.rule: Name each step by its outcome, not its action

page_type_copy_guidance.landing_page: Headline: bold claim or result. Subheadline: who it's for + what they get. CTA: first action, no commitment implied. Social proof: specific numbers over vague adjectives ('Used by 50,000 teams' not 'Loved by many').
page_type_copy_guidance.dashboard: Section labels: noun + verb ('Revenue this month' not just 'Revenue'). KPI labels: concise and consistent. Empty metric: show '—' not '0' for 'not yet measured'.
page_type_copy_guidance.form: Field labels: noun (what it IS, not what to do with it). 'Email address' not 'Enter your email'. Submit: name the outcome.
page_type_copy_guidance.pricing: Plan names: character-based ('Starter', 'Pro', 'Enterprise') or feature-based ('Basic', 'Team', 'Scale'). Feature list: benefits not technical specs. CTA per tier: different copy for each tier.
page_type_copy_guidance.settings: Section headers: plain nouns ('Account', 'Notifications', 'Security'). Dangerous settings: warn in text before the action button. Saved state: 'Saved' not 'Changes saved successfully'.
page_type_copy_guidance.onboarding: Progress label: 'Step 2 of 4' not 'Progress: 50%'. Next button: names the next step, not just 'Next'. Skip option: always present for optional steps as a text link, not a button.
